channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The Trump administration is easing rules on medical marijuana
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said he is immediately moving medical marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III, which includes things like ketamine, Tylenol with codeine, and anabolic steroids.
item.content:encoded: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said he is immediately moving medical marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III, which includes things like ketamine, Tylenol with codeine, and anabolic steroids.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Peace talks between U.S. and Iran at a standstill as Trump extends ceasefire
item.description: President Trump has extended the ceasefire, but Iran says it's not enough if the naval blockade is still in place.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has extended the ceasefire, but Iran says it's not enough if the naval blockade is still in place.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Former deputy commander of Central Command on the future of the Iran war
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin talks with Robert Harward, former deputy commander of Central Command, about U.S. Naval operations around the Strait of Hormuz and implications for the future of the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin talks with Robert Harward, former deputy commander of Central Command, about U.S. Naval operations around the Strait of Hormuz and implications for the future of the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Wildfires — fueled by drought — destroy homes across parts of Georgia and Florida
item.description: Drought conditions are fueling wildfires in South Georgia and Northern Florida that have destroyed homes and caused air quality issues across the region.
item.content:encoded: Drought conditions are fueling wildfires in South Georgia and Northern Florida that have destroyed homes and caused air quality issues across the region.
item.dc:creator: Emily Jones

item.title: World Press Photo announces Photo of the Year 2026
item.description: The executive director of World Press Photo said the image shows the inconsolable grief of children losing their father in a place built for justice. It is a stark and necessary record of family separation following the U.S. reform policies.
item.content:encoded:
The executive director of World Press Photo said the image shows the inconsolable grief of children losing their father in a place built for justice. It is a stark and necessary record of family separation following the U.S. reform policies.

(Image credit: Carol Guzy)
item.dc:creator: Ava Pukatch

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump extends ceasefire, but Iran says it's not enough, Pentagon dismisses Navy secretary, Trump's polling numbers near all-time low as he grapples with Iran war and political fights.
item.content:encoded: Trump extends ceasefire, but Iran says it's not enough, Pentagon dismisses Navy secretary, Trump's polling numbers near all-time low as he grapples with Iran war and political fights.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Warner Bros. Discovery shareholders approve $110B merger with Paramount Skydance
item.description: Paramount CEO David Ellison must now make his case to regulators and a wary Hollywood that the merger is good for the industry.
item.content:encoded:
Paramount CEO David Ellison must now make his case to regulators and a wary Hollywood that the merger is good for the industry.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Nashville works to rebuild its urban forest after losing trees in winter storm
item.description: Nashville lost a lot of trees during an ice storm last winter. Now the city is trying to bring back what was lost.
item.content:encoded: Nashville lost a lot of trees during an ice storm last winter. Now the city is trying to bring back what was lost.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Eggers

item.title: Candidates vying for U.N. secretary-general role face questions
item.description: Who will replace U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres when he steps down this year? Four candidates field questions from member states this week at U.N. headquarters.
item.content:encoded: Who will replace U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres when he steps down this year? Four candidates field questions from member states this week at U.N. headquarters.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Putin's approval rating falls amid state-imposed internet restrictions
item.description: In Russia, expanding state restrictions on the Internet are frustrating daily life for millions — and sending President Putin's approval ratings on a rare downward spiral.
item.content:encoded: In Russia, expanding state restrictions on the Internet are frustrating daily life for millions — and sending President Putin's approval ratings on a rare downward spiral.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes
